subject: [PATCH v7 1/2] mm: bypass mmap_miss heuristic for VM_EXEC readahead

2026-06-02.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel to bypass the mmap_miss heuristic for VM_EXEC readahead mappings. The mmap_miss counter is used to throttle speculative mmap readahead when a file appears to be a random-access workload, but this approach does not fit well with the VM_EXEC path. The patch skips both increments and decrements of the mmap_miss counter for VM_EXEC mappings, matching the existing treatment for VM_SEQ_READ mappings. This change aims to provide more accurate readahead behavior for executable mappings, particularly in configurations where fault-around is disabled.

reviews.author: Pedro Falcato
reviews.summary:
Gave Reviewed-by

Reviewer Pedro Falcato expressed frustration with the ad-hoc nature of filemap/readahead criteria, suggesting that they should be formally documented and clarified to avoid future confusion.
reviews.raw_body:
Just a side comment: I really hate these adhoc criteria all around
filemap/readahead. One day we ought to actually write things down, and
write things in a way that isn't entirely mysterious. I might see if I send
a patch for this down the line...

2026-06-04.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel to bypass the mmap_miss heuristic for VM_EXEC readahead mappings. The mmap_miss counter is used to throttle speculative mmap readahead when a file appears to be a random-access workload, but this approach does not fit well with the VM_EXEC path. The patch skips both increments and decrements of the mmap_miss counter for VM_EXEC mappings, matching the existing treatment for VM_SEQ_READ mappings. This change aims to provide more accurate readahead behavior for executable mappings, particularly in configurations where fault-around is disabled.
